name.text: Identifying element symbol and ionic charge

questiontext.text:
Use the diagram to identify the element symbol and the ionic charge.

Element symbol: [[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]]

Ionic charge: [[input:ans2]] [[validation:ans2]]

Remember: ion notation is written as the element symbol with the charge shown as a superscript.

[[jsxgraph]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard('jxgbox', { boundingbox: [-7.2, 6.8, 7.2, -6.8], keepAspectRatio: true, showCopyright: false, showNavigation: false, axis: false }); var p = {#target_protons#}; var n = {#target_neutrons#}; var e = {#target_electrons#}; var symbol = '{#element_symbol#}'; board.create('circle', [[0, 0], 1], { strokeColor: '#1976d2', strokeWidth: 2, fillOpacity: 0.06, fixed: true }); board.create('circle', [[0, 0], 2], { dash: 2, strokeColor: '#1e88e5', fixed: true }); board.create('circle', [[0, 0], 3.3], { dash: 2, strokeColor: '#1e88e5', fixed: true }); board.create('circle', [[0, 0], 4.6], { dash: 2, strokeColor: '#1e88e5', fixed: true }); board.create('text', [-0.28, 0.14, symbol], { fontSize: 24, fixed: true }); board.create('text', [-6.5, 6.0, function () { return 'Protons: ' + p; }], { fontSize: 14, fixed: true }); board.create('text', [-6.5, 5.4, function () { return 'Neutrons: ' + n; }], { fontSize: 14, fixed: true }); board.create('text', [-6.5, 4.8, function () { return 'Electrons: ' + e; }], { fontSize: 14, fixed: true }); var radii = [2, 3.3, 4.6]; for (let i = 0; i < e; i++) { var shell = 0; var pos = i; var count = Math.min(e, 2); if (i >= 10) { shell = 2; pos = i - 10; count = Math.max(e - 10, 1); } else if (i >= 2) { shell = 1; pos = i - 2; count = Math.max(Math.min(e - 2, 8), 1); } var angle = 2 * Math.PI * pos / count; board.create('point', [ radii[shell] * Math.cos(angle), radii[shell] * Math.sin(angle) ], { size: 3, name: '', withLabel: false, fixed: true, fillColor: '#e46f0e', strokeColor: '#e46f0e' }); } [[/jsxgraph]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked solution

The element symbol is {@element_symbol@} and the charge is {@target_charge@}.

The ion notation is {@element_symbol@}{@ion_charge_sup@}.

Charge is found by subtracting the number of electrons from the number of protons:

\(\text{charge} = \text{protons} - \text{electrons}\)

questiondescription.text: Students identify the element symbol and ionic charge from a nucleus model, then learn how to write the ion notation with a superscript charge.

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

falsefeedback.text: Check the element symbol shown in the center of the diagram.
